input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-full-lifecycle-01-2026-7025b4c1-cluster-a
kubectl -n $NS get pods
echo "---SVC---"
kubectl -n $NS get svc
echo "---VHOSTS---"
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_vhosts
echo "---POLICIES---"
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_policies -p /app
echo "---QUEUES---"
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_queues -p /app name type policy

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-full-lifecycle-01-2026-7025b4c1-cluster-a
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl cluster_status 2>/dev/null | grep -A5 "Running Nodes\|Disk Nodes"
echo "---HEALTH---"
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl node_health_check 2>/dev/null && echo "node0 OK"
echo "---POLICY DETAIL---"
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_policies -p /app

content.text: All requirements are already satisfied, but let me verify mirroring is actually effective (mirrors synced across nodes), since the policy being present doesn't guarantee active mirrors.

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-full-lifecycle-01-2026-7025b4c1-cluster-a
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_queues -p /app name type policy slave_nodes synchronised_slave_nodes 2>/dev/null
